title: Twin it! Part II: high-level political Closing Event marking a Milestone for Europe’s 3D Cultural Heritage Strategy
content:
12 May 2026, Brussels - invitation only event
The Twin it! Part II – 3D for Europe’s culture campaign, invited the Ministries of Culture of the 27 EU Member States to submit at least one 3D digitised heritage asset to the common European data space for cultural heritage, … Continue reading →

title: Cross-Fertilisation process on the topic of Regenerative Tourism Concept
content:
SECreTOUR Networking activities
  Regenera4MED is a project co-funded by the Interreg Euro-MED Programme of the European Commission to help Mediterranean regions shifting from sustainable to regenerative tourism, including restoring ecosystems, culture and community well-being. The Regenera4MED project launched an interesting cross-fertilisation initiative … Continue reading →

title: Heritage at Risk Summer School 2026 – 3D Documentation of Cultural Heritage at Risk: Methods, Tools and Practice.
content:
7–8 May 2026, Hybrid (Ferrara and online)
The Heritage at Risk Summer School 2026, organised in collaboration with the 3D-4CH Project and in the framework of 3DBigDataSpace, brough together experts, researchers, and practitioners to explore this question through advanced 3D documentation methods. Participants gained insights into: European … Continue reading →

title: Use the EUreka3D-XR Toolbox to transform 2D and 3D digital collections into XR storytelling
content:
A new blogpost on the Common European Data Space for Cultural Heritage
A new blogpost was produced and published on the Common European Data Space for Cultural Heritage to disseminate and promote the five open-source digital tools developed by EUreka3D-XR. In the blogpost, the AR Tour Builder, the AR Tour Experience, the … Continue reading →

title: Metaheritage Webinar #5: Exploring EIT Culture & Creativity Support for European SMEs
content:
June 4, 10-11AM
The MetaHeritage Webinar Series continues with its fifth session, focusing on the opportunities offered by EIT Culture & Creativity for European SMEs and organisations working across cultural heritage, tourism, and the creative industries. title: EUreka3D-XR: 3D and XR in the European Digital Cultural Heritage
content:
Open source booklet available on Zenodo
EUreka3D-XR has finalised its booklet “EUreka3D-XR: 3D and XR in the European Digital Cultural Heritage“: the publication describes the EUreka3D competence centre, including the EUreka3D Data Hub, their training and capacity building work, its impact, sustainability and future plans, and … Continue reading →

title: New EUreka3D-XR galleries published on Europeana
content:
Explore the newly aggregated objects!
Following the most recent (and still ongoing) aggregation of new objects digitized in the context of the EUreka3D-XR projects, four new galleries on Europeana were created by Bibracte. Start your journey: The Hidden Side of Bibracte A Gaulish public space: … Continue reading →

title: Building Cultural Bridges, Shaping Shared Futures: The Power of the Europeana Network Association Community
content:
May 25, 2026 (14:30-17.30), Limassol (Cyprus)
On May 25, 2026 the Europeana Network Association (ENA) event will bring together more than 6,000 multi- and interdisciplinary professionals to expand, enrich, and democratise access to Europe’s digital cultural heritage. This event is targeted at the full spectrum of … Continue reading →

title: REMOTE WORK IN EUROPE: Evidence, Impacts, Policy
content:
